title: Intro to Felt Surrogacy

jokes.text: Okay, this awkward silence has been going on for days.
jokes.setup: Cold open with characters sitting in uncomfortable silence
jokes.punchline: Meta-acknowledgment that breaks the fourth wall about the situation
jokes.explanation: Strong meta opening that efficiently establishes the premise while acknowledging the awkwardness directly

jokes.text: Granted, Jeffrey looks amazing when he broods
jokes.setup: Trying to address the group's silence problem
jokes.punchline: Gets distracted by Jeff's attractiveness even while brooding
jokes.explanation: Classic Annie character moment - her crush on Jeff interrupts her logical thinking

dialogue.speaker: Annie
dialogue.line: We need to talk about why nobody's speaking up in meetings. It's like we're all just sitting there in silence, and I don't understand why because communication is so important and—wait, why is Jeff looking at me like that? Does he have something to say? He always has that look when he's about to say something smart and—no, focus Annie. The point is, we need to break this cycle of not talking, and I think if we all just committed to being more vocal and expressive, we could really—is it hot in here? Why is he sitting like that?

jokes.text: Why did you make, have, and bring these?
jokes.setup: Dean Craig appears with puppets that look like each study group member
jokes.punchline: Jeff's incredulous reaction to the absurd situation
jokes.explanation: Jeff's dry delivery and specific word choice captures his exasperation perfectly

dialogue.speaker: Dean Craig
dialogue.line: I've created puppets of all of you! Meet puppet Jeff, puppet Britta, puppet Troy, puppet Abed, puppet Shirley, and puppet Annie!

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: Dean, what the hell are you doing?

dialogue.speaker: Dean Craig
dialogue.line: I thought we could use them for administrative training exercises!

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: You made puppets of us. Without asking. And you're just... showing them to us now.

jokes.text: It's not like I had them on hand for personal use.
jokes.setup: Dean explaining he made the puppets quickly
jokes.punchline: Implies he might normally have puppets of students for personal reasons
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dean moment - his inappropriate oversharing and creepy implications delivered with complete obliviousness

dialogue.speaker: Dean Craig Pelton
dialogue.line: I made these puppets quickly. Normally I take my time with them.

jokes.text: Let me just grab this thing that's definitely not a whip.
jokes.setup: Dean reaching for something off-screen
jokes.punchline: Suspicious denial that draws attention to what he's grabbing
jokes.explanation: Brilliant use of denial as confirmation - the more specific the denial, the funnier it becomes

dialogue.speaker: Dean Craig Pelton
dialogue.line: I'm not doing anything suspicious.

jokes.text: Dean, puppet Kevin here thinks I should have a candy bar right now. No, you know how it winds you up. Fine. We'll split it. All right. Got money, bro? He's not what he seems!
jokes.setup: Troy demonstrating puppet therapy by talking to his puppet
jokes.punchline: Creates an entire personality and conflict for his puppet including paranoid warning
jokes.explanation: Troy's childlike imagination creates a complex puppet personality with internal conflict and mysterious warnings

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: Hey there, little buddy. How are you feeling today?

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: What's that? You're worried about something?

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: Well, don't trust anyone in this room. They're all out to get you. Even me. Especially me.

jokes.text: Although, no one's seen him since he lost his mind in the woods.
jokes.setup: Discussing Pierce's absence from the group
jokes.punchline: Casually mentions Pierce had a complete mental breakdown in the wilderness
jokes.explanation: Shirley's matter-of-fact delivery of disturbing information about Pierce's mental state

dialogue.speaker: Shirley
dialogue.line: Pierce had a complete mental breakdown in the wilderness.

jokes.text: Dean, what happened is between us and Jesus, and Jesus don't snitch.
jokes.setup: Dean asking what happened to cause the awkwardness
jokes.punchline: Shirley's religious reference with street slang about Jesus not being a snitch
jokes.explanation: Perfect Shirley line mixing her religious devotion with urban vernacular in an unexpected way

dialogue.speaker: Shirley
dialogue.line: Jesus ain't no snitch.

jokes.text: But I am a fan of the medium, so... my father is withholding.
jokes.setup: Abed saying he doesn't need puppets to express himself
jokes.punchline: Immediately uses puppet to reveal personal trauma about his father
jokes.explanation: Brilliant contradiction - Abed claims he doesn't need puppets while immediately using one for emotional expression, very meta

dialogue.speaker: Abed
dialogue.line: I don't need puppets to express myself.

dialogue.speaker: Abed
dialogue.line: My father never paid attention to me anyway.

jokes.text: Who are we, Jeff during sex?
jokes.setup: Asking if they'll avoid eye contact forever
jokes.punchline: Specific dig at Jeff's sexual performance and emotional unavailability
jokes.explanation: Sharp, unexpected personal attack from Annie with perfect comedic timing and character insight

dialogue.speaker: Annie
dialogue.line: So what, we're just going to avoid eye contact forever?

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: I mean, that's kind of what we've been doing.

dialogue.speaker: Annie
dialogue.line: Well, it's not going to work because I'm not going anywhere, and frankly, you're not that good at it anyway — the sex thing. You're very... efficient about it. In and out. Emotionally unavailable the whole time.

jokes.text: If we're gonna play this game, then, unlike sex with Britta, we're gonna do it quickly and with a small shred of dignity.
jokes.setup: Jeff agreeing to use the puppets
jokes.punchline: Retaliatory dig at Britta's sexual performance with specific details
jokes.explanation: Jeff's comeback to Annie's insult, maintaining the sexual humor while being characteristically cutting

jokes.text: Jeffrey, dignity is my middle name, and my first name is Dean-nocchio!
jokes.setup: Jeff mentioning dignity
jokes.punchline: Dean's terrible pun combining his name with Pinocchio
jokes.explanation: Classic Dean terrible pun that's funny because of how bad it is and his pride in delivering it

dialogue.speaker: Dean Craig
dialogue.line: Well, if it's dignity you're after, Jeff, just remember: I'm not a real dean, but my nose grows when I lie! Call me Pinocchio Craig!

jokes.text: That's called rubber flubbing, and it's a real problem. Open your eyes, people!
jokes.setup: Explaining her bracelet about rubber waste
jokes.punchline: Made-up term 'rubber flubbing' delivered with passionate activist intensity
jokes.explanation: Brilliant made-up term that sounds plausible, delivered with Britta's characteristic misguided passion

dialogue.speaker: Britta
dialogue.line: This bracelet is about rubber flubbing. It's a serious environmental issue that nobody talks about. Every year, millions of tons of rubber just... flub. Into the ocean. Into the soil. And nobody cares!

jokes.text: Britta said, 'Open your eyes, people.' Square. Nice.
jokes.setup: Britta just said her predictable catchphrase
jokes.punchline: Troy and Abed immediately mark it on their bingo card
jokes.explanation: Excellent meta-joke about sitcom predictability, perfectly executed with immediate payoff

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: Oh, that's bingo!

dialogue.speaker: Abed
dialogue.line: We got another one.

jokes.text: We've been best friends for four years, so we've fallen into a comfortable, repetitive pattern like humorous misunderstandings on Three's Company or girls gossiping on Gossip Girl.
jokes.setup: Explaining why they made the bingo game
jokes.punchline: Direct commentary on sitcom tropes while living in a sitcom
jokes.explanation: Peak Community meta-humor with Abed analyzing their own show's structure using other TV references

dialogue.speaker: Abed
dialogue.line: We made this bingo game to call out all the overused sitcom tropes we keep falling into. You know, the contrived conflicts, the convenient resolutions, the way everything wraps up in 22 minutes.

dialogue.speaker: Abed
dialogue.line: It's ironic, really. We're a sitcom making fun of sitcom tropes while being unable to escape being a sitcom ourselves.

jokes.text: This game is gay. Square.
jokes.setup: Jeff complaining about the bingo game
jokes.punchline: Troy immediately marks it as another predictable Jeff response
jokes.explanation: Great callback showing how predictable Jeff's responses are, proving the bingo game's point

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: This bingo game is ridiculous. I'm not participating in some childish game night nonsense.

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: Bingo! *marks card* Jeff complains about group activities.

jokes.text: That's not a very Christian thing to do. Square.
jokes.setup: Shirley's response to the mockery
jokes.punchline: Another immediate bingo square marked
jokes.explanation: Perfectly predictable Shirley response that proves the bingo game's effectiveness

dialogue.speaker: Shirley
dialogue.line: Another immediate bingo square marked

jokes.text: Can we go back to studying now? Square. I don't like this game. Me neither. I think it's mean. There's no point. Yeah, it's insulting. Okay, stop. Square.
jokes.setup: Multiple predictable responses happening rapidly
jokes.punchline: Cascade of bingo squares being marked in quick succession
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation showing how predictable they all are, building to comedic crescendo

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: Okay, here we go. I'm going to say something, and you're all going to react exactly how I expect.

dialogue.speaker: Annie
dialogue.line: We always do that!

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: Yeah, we're pretty predictable.

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: Exactly. Which is why I made this.
[Cascade of bingo squares being marked in quick succession]

jokes.text: But to be fair, there's not many things you could be stuck in. Elevators. Bad marriages. A peat bog. On a flight in the middle seat. Time loops. The bottom of a well. Your own emotions. Quicksand. Okay, stop! Square.
jokes.setup: Discussing being stuck in a rut
jokes.punchline: Rapid-fire list of increasingly absurd things to be stuck in, ending with another bingo square
jokes.explanation: Good escalation from literal to metaphorical things to be stuck in, capped with bingo payoff

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: We're stuck in a rut.

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: Stuck in a rut? I'm stuck in a pit!

dialogue.speaker: Abed
dialogue.line: I'm stuck in a well.

dialogue.speaker: Pierce
dialogue.line: I'm stuck in a tree.

dialogue.speaker: Shirley
dialogue.line: I'm stuck in a love trapezoid.

dialogue.speaker: Annie
dialogue.line: I'm stuck in a bingo square.

jokes.text: They all really typify the diversity we have here at Greendale. Uh, ruggedly handsome leading man... a mom, an activist, a perfect student, a lifelong learner, a sidekick, and Abed.
jokes.setup: Dean describing the study group to prospective students
jokes.punchline: Reduces everyone to TV tropes, then gives up and just says 'and Abed'
jokes.explanation: Great meta-joke about TV character archetypes with Abed as the uncategorizable one

dialogue.speaker: Dean Craig
dialogue.line: We have the leader, the smart one, the attractive one, the quirky one, the cynic, and the lovable idiot.

dialogue.speaker: Dean Craig
dialogue.line: And Abed.

jokes.text: Yeah, prepare to be pop-popped! Aw, he's a national treasure.
jokes.setup: Dean introducing Magnitude to prospective students
jokes.punchline: Dean's over-the-top appreciation for Magnitude's one-note character
jokes.explanation: Dean's sincere pride in Greendale's most limited student is endearingly absurd

dialogue.speaker: Dean Craig
dialogue.line: This is Magnitude, one of our most celebrated students. He has a very special talent.

dialogue.speaker: Magnitude
dialogue.line: Pop pop!

dialogue.speaker: Dean Craig
dialogue.line: You see? Magnificent. The way he says it with such conviction, such passion. Pop pop! It's not just a sound, it's a philosophy. It's a way of life. Some say he invented the word. Others say the word invented him.

jokes.text: Hey, whose dad has a vintage Ferrari?
jokes.setup: Reference to taking a day off like Ferris Bueller
jokes.punchline: Troy's literal interpretation focusing on the car aspect
jokes.explanation: Troy misses the point of the reference and focuses on the material aspect

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: You know what? I'm taking a day off. I'm gonna be like Ferris Bueller.

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: I'm gonna take his dad's car out for a spin.

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: Wait, whose car am I taking?

jokes.text: Abed, a pop culture reference is more of the same.
jokes.setup: They're trying to break out of their predictable patterns
jokes.punchline: Calling out pop culture references in a show built on pop culture references
jokes.explanation: Self-aware commentary on the show's own tendency toward pop culture references

dialogue.speaker: Annie
dialogue.line: We're always making pop culture references.

dialogue.speaker: Britta
dialogue.line: So? That's what makes us funny.

dialogue.speaker: Abed
dialogue.line: We're literally a show about making pop culture references while commenting on making pop culture references.

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: That's very meta.

dialogue.speaker: Abed
dialogue.line: I know. I referenced the concept of referencing.

jokes.text: ♪ Could we travel through time? or maybe see France? or anywhere else naked ladies will dance? ♪
jokes.setup: Musical number about adventure possibilities
jokes.punchline: Troy's priorities quickly shift to strip clubs
jokes.explanation: Troy's juvenile priorities coming through even in a fantastical context

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: We could go to strip clubs!

jokes.text: ♪ I'd rather stay at Greendale ♪
jokes.setup: Everyone singing about adventure and escape
jokes.punchline: Pierce preferring to stay at the terrible community college
jokes.explanation: Pierce's contrarian nature and fear of change coming through in song

dialogue.speaker: Pierce
dialogue.line: I'm staying. I like it here.

jokes.text: ♪ Pierce, bring your dentures ♪
jokes.setup: Excited song about hot air balloon adventure
jokes.punchline: Practical reminder about Pierce's old age needs
jokes.explanation: Great juxtaposition of adventure excitement with mundane old-age practicalities

dialogue.speaker: Annie
dialogue.line: Oh my god, a hot air balloon ride! This is going to be amazing!

dialogue.speaker: Pierce
dialogue.line: I'm in. But we need to make sure there's a comfortable seat. My back isn't what it used to be.

dialogue.speaker: Annie
dialogue.line: Pierce, we're going to be floating thousands of feet in the air!

dialogue.speaker: Pierce
dialogue.line: Exactly. Which is why I need to bring my orthopedic pillow and a catheter bag.

dialogue.speaker: Annie
dialogue.line: ...Maybe we should just go to the movies instead.

jokes.text: ♪ I'd rather fly to Vegas ♪
jokes.setup: Others singing about heaven
jokes.punchline: Pierce's more earthly, vice-oriented destination preference
jokes.explanation: Typical Pierce response prioritizing gambling and vice over spiritual aspirations

dialogue.speaker: Pierce
dialogue.line: I'm going to hell.

jokes.text: From now on, no more singing in the balloon.
jokes.setup: Just finished elaborate musical number
jokes.punchline: Guide's practical response to their musical outburst
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan response that acknowledges the absurdity of the musical number

dialogue.speaker: Balloon Guide
dialogue.line: Okay, so... you guys done? Because we still have seventeen more floors to go.

jokes.text: You could have died. You could have died. How could you almost let him die?
jokes.setup: Learning about Jeff's balloon accident
jokes.punchline: Dean's over-the-top emotional reaction focused specifically on Jeff
jokes.explanation: Dean's obsessive concern for Jeff specifically, ignoring that everyone was in danger

dialogue.speaker: Dean Pelton
dialogue.line: Oh my God! Jeff! Are you alright? A balloon accident?! This is the most tragic thing that's ever happened at Greendale! Well, besides the time a student got their hand stuck in a vending machine. And that other time with the fire. And that incident in the cafeteria. But THIS? This is right up there!

jokes.text: Come on, people. I can't do this alone. You can't do it at all.
jokes.setup: Pierce trying to help with the balloon crisis
jokes.punchline: Jeff's blunt assessment of Pierce's uselessness
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jeff comeback with efficient, brutal honesty about Pierce's capabilities

dialogue.speaker: Pierce
dialogue.line: I'm here to help with the balloon crisis!

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: Pierce, you're not helping. You're just standing there being useless.

jokes.text: If we fly to heaven, please don't tell my grandpa about me and Britta.
jokes.setup: Facing potential death in balloon
jokes.punchline: Troy's worried about posthumous judgment of his relationship
jokes.explanation: Troy's childlike worry about family approval even in crisis situation

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: If I die in this balloon, people are gonna judge me for dating a woman in a wheelchair!

dialogue.speaker: Abed
dialogue.line: That's not what they'll judge you for.

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: What will they judge me for?

dialogue.speaker: Abed
dialogue.line: Dying in a balloon.

jokes.text: I can't even see the Landing Strip or any of the other nudie bars.
jokes.setup: Everyone panicking about being lost and high up
jokes.punchline: Pierce uses strip clubs as geographical landmarks
jokes.explanation: Perfect Pierce moment using his vice-based navigation system during crisis

dialogue.speaker: Pierce
dialogue.line: Relax! I know exactly where we are. We're two blocks south of the Diamond Club, three blocks north of Señoritas, and directly above the old Platinum Palace.

jokes.text: That's international airspace. We're literally above the law. Jeff can marry any man he wants.
jokes.setup: Realizing they're very high up
jokes.punchline: Annie's misunderstanding of international law and marriage rights
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurd logic about international airspace leading to marriage freedom

dialogue.speaker: Annie
dialogue.line: Wait, if we're this high up, doesn't that mean we're in international airspace? I read that in international airspace, anyone can marry anyone!

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: Annie, that's not a real law.

dialogue.speaker: Annie
dialogue.line: Well, it should be! It's the perfect loophole!

jokes.text: So does gravity, Shirley. And you know who invented gravity, right?
jokes.setup: Shirley attributing their descent to prayer
jokes.punchline: Pierce implying he invented gravity (or setting up to claim credit)
jokes.explanation: Classic Pierce narcissism, ready to take credit for fundamental physics

dialogue.speaker: Shirley
dialogue.line: We're descending because I've been praying.

dialogue.speaker: Pierce
dialogue.line: Well, I invented gravity, so technically you're praying to me.

jokes.text: Oh, damn you, gravity! Damn you, prayer! Damn you, Vicki!
jokes.setup: Everyone cursing different things as they crash
jokes.punchline: Troy randomly cursing a minor character for no reason
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation ending with Troy's random, inexplicable blame of background character

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: Aaargh!

dialogue.speaker: Shirley
dialogue.line: Oh God!

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: You jerk, Custodian Romero!

jokes.text: Has anyone else noticed Professor Duncan hasn't been around for a long time?
jokes.setup: Random moment during crisis
jokes.punchline: Fourth wall break about missing cast member
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta-joke about actor availability/contract issues disguised as character observation

dialogue.speaker: Annie
dialogue.line: Hey, where's Shirley?

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: I don't know, she's been missing all episode.

dialogue.speaker: Annie
dialogue.line: This is like when Yvette left the show.

dialogue.speaker: Abed
dialogue.line: We're doing a bit about a cast member leaving? That's very meta, even for us.

jokes.text: Pierce, no! Bring it to me strong, old man monkey strength!
jokes.setup: Pierce trying to help soften their landing
jokes.punchline: Pierce's bizarre self-encouragement using monkey strength
jokes.explanation: Pierce's delusional self-motivation with absurd animal strength reference

dialogue.speaker: Pierce
dialogue.line: Come on, Jeff, we can do this. I'm gonna channel my inner monkey. Monkeys are strong. I'm gonna be like a monkey.

dialogue.speaker: Pierce
dialogue.line: Oo oo oo oo oo! Oo oo oo oo oo!

jokes.text: I'm so sorry. When you hurt, I hurt.
jokes.setup: Apologizing for interruption
jokes.punchline: Over-the-top emotional connection specifically with Jeff
jokes.explanation: Classic Dean inappropriate emotional intimacy with Jeff

dialogue.speaker: Dean Craig
dialogue.line: I'm so sorry to interrupt, but I just had to tell you something, Jeff. When I look at you, I don't just see a student. I see a kindred spirit. A reflection of my own dreams and aspirations. You have this way of making me feel... understood.

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: Dean, that's... that's very sweet, but—

dialogue.speaker: Dean Craig
dialogue.line: No, let me finish. I've never felt this kind of connection with anyone before. It's like we're two souls orbiting the same sun, Jeff. And I just needed you to know that.

jokes.text: I'm alive. Square.
jokes.setup: Everyone confirming they survived the crash
jokes.punchline: Even survival gets marked on the bingo card
jokes.explanation: Great callback showing how even dramatic moments become predictable patterns

dialogue.speaker: Abed
dialogue.line: Even survival gets marked on the bingo card

jokes.text: We need to start a prison system.
jokes.setup: Discussing survival priorities
jokes.punchline: Pierce's immediate jump to authoritarian control systems
jokes.explanation: Perfect Pierce response showing his authoritarian tendencies in crisis

dialogue.speaker: Pierce
dialogue.line: When society collapses, the first thing we need is a strong leader to maintain order. Someone decisive. Someone who won't hesitate to make the hard choices.

dialogue.speaker: Pierce
dialogue.line: And obviously, that person is me.

jokes.text: We'll be stranded here forever, and I've never seen blue man group!
jokes.setup: Panicking about being stranded
jokes.punchline: Troy's specific regret about missing a Vegas show
jokes.explanation: Troy's priorities showing his fear of missing out on specific cultural experiences

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: I can't believe we're stranded! We're never going to make it to Vegas!

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: Do you know what's playing in Vegas right now? Chippendales! And I'm going to miss it!

jokes.text: I did see blue man group. I just didn't get it. Why can't they talk? They have so much in common.
jokes.setup: Troy's regret about Blue Man Group
jokes.punchline: Pierce's confusion about the performance art concept
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Pierce misunderstanding of performance art, thinking they should talk because they're all blue

dialogue.speaker: Pierce
dialogue.line: Blue Man Group? I thought you said you went to see Blue Man, like, a guy who was blue.

dialogue.speaker: Pierce
dialogue.line: I was expecting more of a medical documentary.

dialogue.speaker: Pierce
dialogue.line: You know, like a case study.

jokes.text: And I thought my hair looked fake.
jokes.setup: Meeting the mountain man with presumably bad hair
jokes.punchline: Annie's self-deprecating comment about her own hair
jokes.explanation: Annie's insecurity about her appearance even when meeting someone with worse hair

dialogue.speaker: Annie
dialogue.line: At least my hair doesn't look like it was styled by a tornado in a flannel factory.

jokes.text: How did you know my name is Pierce? It's embroidered on your Greendale shirt you got at the student store. How'd you know I got it at the student store? I'm a touch psychic.
jokes.setup: Pierce amazed that mountain man knows his name
jokes.punchline: Obvious explanation followed by Pierce's continued amazement and absurd psychic claim
jokes.explanation: Great escalation of Pierce's obliviousness to obvious explanations

dialogue.speaker: Mountain Man
dialogue.line: I know your name because you're wearing a name tag.

dialogue.speaker: Pierce
dialogue.line: How did you...? That's incredible!

dialogue.speaker: Mountain Man
dialogue.line: It literally says 'Pierce' right here.

dialogue.speaker: Pierce
dialogue.line: You must be psychic. I'm going to tell everyone you can read minds.

jokes.text: So this is how Greendale graduates end up: As transient mountain men. Huh. Not as bad as I thought.
jokes.setup: Meeting a Greendale graduate living in the woods
jokes.punchline: Jeff's low expectations being exceeded by homelessness
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jeff cynicism about Greendale's value, with homelessness exceeding expectations

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: So you're living out here in the woods?

dialogue.speaker: Homeless Graduate
dialogue.line: Yeah, I graduated from Greendale and this is where life took me.

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: Wow, that's... actually better than I expected.

jokes.text: Hi, Troy.
jokes.setup: Musical number about being free in the woods
jokes.punchline: A moose apparently greets Troy by name
jokes.explanation: Surreal moment of nature personification greeting Troy specifically

dialogue.speaker: Moose
dialogue.line: Troy!

jokes.text: ♪ my hand just made a rainbow ♪ ♪ Magic, magic ♪ ♪ well, these magical berries put us in a drug seq-- ♪
jokes.setup: Characters experiencing hallucinogenic effects
jokes.punchline: Abed breaking the fourth wall by identifying they're in a drug sequence
jokes.explanation: Peak meta-humor with Abed recognizing and naming the TV trope they're currently in

dialogue.speaker: Abed
dialogue.line: We're in a drug sequence.

jokes.text: Dean! There is a fire in the cafeteria. Garrett, not now!
jokes.setup: Dean focused on his therapy session
jokes.punchline: Dismissing actual emergency for his pet project
jokes.explanation: Dean's misplaced priorities dismissing real emergency for puppet therapy

dialogue.speaker: Dean Craig
dialogue.line: I'm sorry, I can't deal with this right now. I'm in the middle of my therapy session.

dialogue.speaker: Garrett
dialogue.line: Dean, there's an actual emergency—

dialogue.speaker: Dean Craig
dialogue.line: Garrett, some of us are working on our emotional infrastructure here. This outfit isn't going to curate itself.

jokes.text: Your skin is so beautiful and soft. It's like felt.
jokes.setup: Characters under influence of berries
jokes.punchline: Annie describing skin as felt, referencing the puppet episode format
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta-reference connecting the drug experience to the puppet framing device

dialogue.speaker: Annie
dialogue.line: My skin feels like felt. Are we puppets? Is this a puppet episode?

jokes.text: I was at the grocery store with my kids, and I thought I saw Andre walk by with another woman... I ended up tailing them all the way out of town. It wasn't even Andre. The horrible part is I forgot my kids and left them all night in the grocery store.
jokes.setup: Shirley confessing her terrible secret
jokes.punchline: Abandoning her children overnight due to paranoid jealousy about wrong person
jokes.explanation: Dark character revelation showing Shirley's jealousy overriding maternal instincts

dialogue.speaker: Shirley
dialogue.line: I abandoned my children overnight.

dialogue.speaker: Shirley
dialogue.line: It was due to paranoid jealousy about the wrong person.

jokes.text: treating me like Judas, judging me like Judy. You know, Judge Judy. She judges people.
jokes.setup: Shirley feeling judged by the group
jokes.punchline: Awkward biblical/pop culture pun with unnecessary explanation
jokes.explanation: Shirley's attempt at clever wordplay falls flat, made worse by explaining it

dialogue.speaker: Shirley
dialogue.line: You know what? I'm tired of feeling like you all judge me for my beliefs. It's like you think I'm some kind of... Jesus take the wheel... of my own life decisions.

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: That's not a pun, Shirley.

dialogue.speaker: Shirley
dialogue.line: Well, it is if you think about it. Jesus, take the wheel? Like, he's taking control? But also it's a song by Carrie Underwood? So it's funny because it's both religious AND contemporary pop culture at the same time.

jokes.text: Those berries made us real talky but not real listen-y.
jokes.setup: Realizing no one remembers anyone else's secrets
jokes.punchline: Folksy explanation of the drug's selective effects on communication
jokes.explanation: Clever observation about the berries' effects using playful language

dialogue.speaker: Annie
dialogue.line: Wait, so no one remembers anyone else's secrets? How is that even possible?

dialogue.speaker: Shirley
dialogue.line: Well, honey, it's like my grandmother used to say: a drug that makes you forget what other people told you is like a sieve that only lets water through but keeps the dirt.

dialogue.speaker: Annie
dialogue.line: That doesn't make any sense.

dialogue.speaker: Shirley
dialogue.line: Well, it's selective, sweetheart. It washes away gossip but leaves your own business squeaky clean.

jokes.text: We're in the clear. ♪ We're in the clear ♪ I'm a genius! ♪ We're in the clear ♪ Yeah! ♪ We're in the clear ♪
jokes.setup: Realizing their secrets are safe
jokes.punchline: Spontaneous celebration breaking into song
jokes.explanation: Group celebration that naturally breaks into musical format

dialogue.speaker: Annie
dialogue.line: Wait, you mean nobody's telling anyone?

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: Not a soul.

dialogue.speaker: Britta
dialogue.line: We're actually keeping this secret.

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: This calls for a celebration!

dialogue.speaker: Everyone
dialogue.line: ♪ Our secrets are safe, safe, safe! ♪

dialogue.speaker: Everyone
dialogue.line: ♪ Nobody has to know our shame! ♪

jokes.text: Um, don't mind me. I'm sure you guys can get on with your lives without the worst mom ever. Sounds good. Anyone up for a movie?
jokes.setup: Shirley's dramatic exit as worst mother ever
jokes.punchline: Abed's matter-of-fact acceptance and immediate topic change
jokes.explanation: Perfect Abed response - completely literal and emotionally tone-deaf to Shirley's drama

dialogue.speaker: Shirley
dialogue.line: I'm the worst mother in the world! I don't deserve to live!

dialogue.speaker: Abed
dialogue.line: Okay. So, did you guys see that new Cougar Town?

jokes.text: ♪ I said it didn't bother me But truthfully it did I promised him I'd make it to a baseball game he had But I bailed and never called again And now I'm just exactly like my dad ♪
jokes.setup: Jeff's confession about dating a woman with a child
jokes.punchline: Realizing he's become the absent father figure he resented
jokes.explanation: Powerful character moment revealing Jeff's fear of becoming his abandoning father

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: I didn't sign up to be a dad. I'm not equipped for this.

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: And then it hit me — I've become exactly what I hated. The guy who's never around, making excuses, letting someone down.

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: I'm the absent father. Except I'm not even the father.

jokes.text: ♪ I am an activist That's always been my choice Truth is I've never voted Except when I watch The Voice ♪
jokes.setup: Britta's identity as political activist
jokes.punchline: Admits she doesn't vote except for reality TV singing competition
jokes.explanation: Perfect Britta hypocrisy - passionate activist who doesn't engage in basic civic duty

dialogue.speaker: Britta
dialogue.line: I'm a political activist. I care deeply about social justice and changing the system.

dialogue.speaker: Britta
dialogue.line: I don't actually vote... except for American Idol. I take that very seriously.

jokes.text: ♪ I let Cornwallis rub my feet to give me all the answers to a test ♪
jokes.setup: Annie's confession about academic struggles
jokes.punchline: Prostituting herself (feet) to historical figure for test answers
jokes.explanation: Annie's academic desperation leading to inappropriate exchange with historical figure

dialogue.speaker: Annie
dialogue.line: I may have prostituted myself to a historical figure for test answers.

jokes.text: ♪ I caused the Greendale fire of '03 55 acres went up in a blaze All because I burned an ant hill ♪
jokes.setup: Troy confessing to causing environmental disaster
jokes.punchline: Massive fire started from childish ant hill burning
jokes.explanation: Perfect Troy secret - enormous consequences from childish, thoughtless action

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: I may have started a massive fire.

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: I was trying to burn down an ant hill like I used to do when I was a kid.

jokes.text: ♪ I've never slept with the great Eartha Kitt We dry humped inside of her tour bus ♪
jokes.setup: Pierce's confession about celebrity encounter
jokes.punchline: Clarifying he didn't sleep with her, just dry humped on tour bus
jokes.explanation: Classic Pierce overshare with specific inappropriate details about celebrity encounter

dialogue.speaker: Pierce
dialogue.line: I didn't sleep with her, we just dry humped on the tour bus.

jokes.text: Well, uh... the level to which we respect you as a political activist has definitely not changed.
jokes.setup: Britta asking if they respect her less as activist
jokes.punchline: Backhanded compliment implying they never respected her activism
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly constructed insult disguised as reassurance - they never respected her activism

dialogue.speaker: Britta
dialogue.line: Do you guys respect me less as an activist?

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: No, no. We respect you the same amount as we always have.

jokes.text: Knowledge is a party.
jokes.setup: Students leaving after successful therapy
jokes.punchline: Dean's awkward attempt at inspirational catchphrase
jokes.explanation: Classic Dean awkward attempt at being inspirational and memorable

dialogue.speaker: Dean Craig Pelton
dialogue.line: As you leave, remember: you're all... streets ahead!

jokes.text: Once again, I help fix everyone's problems only to go home alone. Who says you have to be alone? Jeffrey.
jokes.setup: Dean lamenting his loneliness after helping others
jokes.punchline: Jeff's unexpected offer of companionship, Dean's breathless response
jokes.explanation: Dean's fantasy moment seeming to come true with Jeff's apparent invitation

dialogue.speaker: Dean Craig
dialogue.line: I do so much for people, and yet I'm so alone.

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: Hey, you want to get a drink?

dialogue.speaker: Dean Craig
dialogue.line: Really?

jokes.text: What are we doing here? / Yeah. / No, Gollum, no!
jokes.setup: Dean and Jeff in ambiguous intimate situation
jokes.punchline: Dean's Lord of the Rings reference during inappropriate moment
jokes.explanation: Weird Dean moment mixing fantasy with pop culture references. The Gollum reference is random but fits his character's tendency toward inappropriate references.

jokes.text: There's so much skin! So much skin came off! What happened? Did he knock it off? What'd I miss?
jokes.setup: Behind-the-scenes puppet filming
jokes.punchline: Apparent puppet malfunction with skin/felt coming off
jokes.explanation: Physical comedy from puppet/costume malfunction during filming

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: Why is the puppet's skin coming off?

dialogue.speaker: Abed
dialogue.line: That's not a malfunction. That's the director's vision.

dialogue.speaker: Britta
dialogue.line: Shouldn't we stop filming?

dialogue.speaker: Abed
dialogue.line: No. Keep rolling. This is art.

tmdb_overview: Dean Pelton prompts the study group to use puppets to relive the traumatic events of a recent adventure.
insight: Meta-layered character comedy sustains 81.8 with 179 total jokes across 64-minute runtime.
